text: <sup>[This is being written by]</sup> the elder to my dear <sup>[friend]</sup> Gaius, whom I love in the truth. <sup>[Note: This probably means “whom I truly love”]</sup>.

<sup>[My]</sup> dearly loved <sup>[friend]</sup>, I am praying that you will do well in everything <sup>[materially (?)]</sup>, and that you will have <sup>[good]</sup> health, just as your soul <sup>[i.e., your spiritual life]</sup> is doing well.

For I was very happy when some of the brothers came and told me that you are being <sup>[faithful]</sup> in the truth <sup>[i.e., the word of God]</sup>, and that you are living according to it, <sup>[as well]</sup>.
I do not have any greater joy than to hear that my <sup>[spiritual]</sup> children are living according to the truth.

Dear <sup>[friend]</sup>, you are accomplishing a faithful work whenever you do something for the brothers <sup>[traveling in the Lord’s work]</sup>, especially when they are strangers <sup>[to you]</sup>.

These people testify about your love <sup>[for them]</sup> before the <sup>[assembled]</sup> church. You will be doing a good thing to send them on their journey in a way that is pleasing to God <sup>[i.e., by furnishing them with travel expenses for their ministry]</sup>.
For they went out, <sup>[ministering]</sup> for the sake of <sup>[Jesus’]</sup> name, without taking <sup>[any money]</sup> from Gentiles <sup>[i.e., non-Christians]</sup>.
Therefore, we should show hospitality to such men, so that we can be fellow-workers <sup>[with them]</sup> in <sup>[the cause of]</sup> the truth.

I wrote a letter to the church <sup>[there]</sup>, but Diotrephes, who loves to be first <sup>[in importance]</sup> among the members <sup>[of the body there]</sup>, does not receive us <sup>[i.e., he did not listen to or acknowledge the authority of us apostles]</sup>.

So, if I come <sup>[to visit you]</sup>, I will remind you of what he is doing, <sup>[such as]</sup> using wicked words to talk nonsense against us <sup>[apostles]</sup>. And, not being satisfied with that, he <sup>[also]</sup> refuses to welcome the <sup>[other]</sup> brothers, and prevents <sup>[other]</sup> people from doing so <sup>[i.e., from welcoming these brothers]</sup>, and even kicks them out of the church <sup>[for trying to]</sup>.

Dear <sup>[friend]</sup>, do not imitate what is evil, but <sup>[only]</sup> what is good. The person who does what is good is <sup>[a child]</sup> of God; the one who does evil has not seen God <sup>[spiritually]</sup>.

Demetrius has been given <sup>[a favorable]</sup> commendation by everyone, and <sup>[is commended]</sup> by the truth itself. We <sup>[apostles]</sup> also give him <sup>[a favorable]</sup> commendation and you know that our testimony <sup>[about these things]</sup> is true.
I had many things <sup>[I wanted]</sup> to write you about, but I do not want to do it with pen and ink,
because I hope to see you soon, and <sup>[then]</sup> we can speak face to face.
